transcript: Speaker A: One cold coffee, aur ek expresso. [One cold coffee and one espresso.] Speaker B: Bro athe Espresso aanu. [Bro, that's Espresso.] Speaker A: Haan athe Express-o. [Yes, Express-o.] Speaker B: Nee traina order cheyyunnath atho kaapiyo? [Are you ordering a train or coffee?] Speaker A: Njan mindil pronunciation sheryayi varum, pakshe vayil ninnu purathu vere pole purathu varum. [In my mind, the pronunciation comes out right, but from my mouth, it comes out differently.] Speaker B: Enikkum ingane thanne aayirunnu. Athukond njan speaking practice start cheythu. Ithil nee paraya mathrala, AI udane parayum entha mistake enn. [I was also like this. That's why I started speaking practice. In this, you don't just speak, the AI immediately tells you what the mistake is.] Speaker B: Pinne best part entha ariyo? Ithu real life situationsil practice cheyyippikkum. Cafe, meeting, interviews, jeevithathil awkward aakunna situations ellam. Daily 15 minutes samsarikunna practice. Pinne pinne confidence naturally varum. [And do you know what the best part is? It makes you practice in real-life situations. Cafe, meetings, interviews, all situations that make life awkward. Daily 15 minutes of speaking practice. Then confidence will come naturally.] Speaker A: One Espresso. Speaker B: Haiva, ippo ivan LinkedIn captions pole samsarikunnu. [Wow, now he speaks like LinkedIn captions.] Speaker A: First 20 questions free aanu. Ini awkward silence venda. SpeakX try cheyyu. Speak Confidently. Download SpeakX. [First 20 questions are free. No more awkward silence. Try SpeakX. Speak Confidently. Download SpeakX.]

summary: A man struggles to pronounce 'espresso' correctly at a cafe, prompting his friend to recommend the SpeakX app. The app uses AI to correct pronunciation and offers real-life scenario practice. The ad ends with a call to action to download SpeakX for confident English speaking.
